reasoning: The 360-day average is 11.25 per 30 days. For the 30 days prior to the resolution date to resolve positively, a count of 12 or more is required. Under a Poisson or slightly overdispersed distribution with mean ~11.25, the probability of strictly exceeding the mean is typically around 40-45% due to the median commonly being slightly lower than the mean. Given a potential gradual increase in remote violence (e.g., drone and artillery use by M23 and FARDC, and IEDs by ADF), I estimate the probability of reaching 12 or more events to be around 46%.
